title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich police investigate alleged threat by a middle school student against classmates following an alert from a safety app.
The student in question is not attending school during the investigation, ensuring safety, according to school officials.
The Cape & Islands District Attorney's Office is collaborating with the Sandwich Police in the ongoing investigation.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, was convicted of first-degree murder in a 2015 Dorchester apartment invasion, resulting in a man's death.
Arrington and accomplices entered the wrong apartment, killed Richard Long, and attacked his fiancée during a botched robbery.
The retrial used iPhone location evidence; Arrington faces life without parole, with sentencing set for Tuesday.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of NBC's 'American Ninja Warrior' features 18 New England competitors, including 12 from Massachusetts.
Veteran competitors Addy Herman and Noah Meunier, both from Massachusetts, are expected to have standout performances this season.
New England boasts at least 10 ninja gyms, contributing to the region's strong presence in the competition.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish soccer fans have been visiting Boston for the World Cup, bringing a charming international presence to the city.
Boston-area restaurant The Haven hosted many Scottish fans, offering traditional Scottish cuisine like haggis during their stay.
Meredith Goldstein invites readers to share experiences about any romantic connections made with Scottish visitors during the event.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Shaolin Soccer" (2001) combines soccer with Shaolin kung fu, offering a comedic sports adventure available on Paramount+, Tubi, and more.
"Diamantino" (2018) is a political satire about a Cristiano Ronaldo-like star, streaming on Kanopy and Hoopla.
"Mike Bassett: England Manager" (2001) satirizes the world of English soccer, with appearances from Pele and Ronaldo Nazario on The Roku Channel.

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Trump administration is considering rolling back maritime slow zones aimed at protecting North Atlantic right whales.
Conservationists fear changes to rules could increase vessel strikes, risking the population of about 380 right whales.
Public responses to potential deregulatory action exceeded 29,000, with NOAA evaluating whale-detection technology implications.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings, striking out seven and allowing one hit, against the Yankees at Fenway Park.
Despite feeling unwell with body aches and fever, Tolle's performance helped the Red Sox secure a 6-1 victory.
Tolle has maintained a 2.78 ERA over 12 starts this season, showcasing dominance particularly against the Yankees.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Celtics consider trading Derrick White and Baylor Scheierman for Trey Murphy to pair with Tatum and Brown.
Celtics explore center upgrades through trades or free agency, with possible targets including Mitchell Robinson and Zach Collins.
Boston may trade Sam Hauser for salary cap flexibility, allowing focus on developing younger players like Hugo Gonzalez.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins acquired JJ Peterka from Utah Mammoth in exchange for two first-round picks, including a conditional pick from the Brad Marchand trade.
Peterka, a 24-year-old German winger, has averaged 26 goals per season in the last three years and is known for his speed and scoring ability.
Peterka and his family have built a hockey rink in Buchbach, Germany, to promote hockey and provide facilities for young players.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown garners trade interest from Portland Trailblazers and Detroit Pistons, according to reports.
Portland Trailblazers could offer a mix of young players and draft picks for Brown, with interest in acquiring top talent.
Detroit Pistons consider a sign-and-trade involving Jalen Duren for Brown, exploring potential moves for the forward.
